title: London Stock Exchange to Introduce Nonstop Trading Next Year
summary: The London Stock Exchange will open a new venue outside of its regular market hours in a move to offer “near-continuous trading” in the working week.

title: Gold Rises on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Middle East Clashes
summary: Gold touched its highest level in a week on dip-buying as traders monitored developments in the Middle East for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.

title: Indonesia Markets First Panda Bonds as It Diversifies Funding
summary: Indonesia has begun marketing its inaugural yuan-denominated bonds in China’s domestic debt market, as Southeast Asia’s largest economy seeks to diversify funding sources amid currency volatility and fiscal challenges.

title: Goldman Says Brent Could Top $120 If Hormuz Disruptions Persist
summary: Brent could rally to more than $120 a barrel by the fourth quarter if disruptions of the Strait of Hormuz persist, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., although that’s not the bank’s base case.

title: Iran War Forces Cash-Strapped Asian Nations to Buy Expensive LNG
summary: Pakistan and Bangladesh were forced to buy some of their most expensive liquefied natural gas shipments in years as the Middle East conflict chokes supply, straining government finances and prompting both countries to rethink their reliance on the super-chilled fuel.

title: Another Tanker Hit in Hormuz as Houthis Add to Regional Risks
summary: Another tanker was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as renewed hostilities empty the waterway, while a threat by Houthi rebels to blockade Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea heightened regional maritime risks.

title: Stocks Rise as Chipmakers Extend Rebound, Oil Dips: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks rose for the first time in four days as investors returned to chipmakers, driving a rebound in the sector after the recent selloff. Oil declined.

title: China Unleashes Broad State Support to Halt Tech Stock Selloff
summary: China is mounting one of its broadest efforts in years to steady the stock market, with regulators, state-backed investors, insurers and asset managers all moving to shore up confidence after a selloff in technology shares.

title: FTSE 100 Live: Pound Steady After Healey Named Chancellor
Merz Gets a Chance to Reset His Chancellorship After Ally Quits
summary: The 48-hour firestorm that took down one of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s key parliamentary allies has given him a window to tighten his grip on the policymaking apparatus.

title: Palm Slips from Month-High as Weak Crude Dents Biofuel Appeal
summary: Palm oil fell from a near one-month high following weakness in the crude and vegetable oil complex, denting the appeal of biofuels.

title: SBI Funds Rises 8.5% in Debut After $1 Billion India IPO
summary: SBI Funds Management Ltd. shares rose 8.5% in their Mumbai debut Tuesday after investors piled into the company’s $1 billion initial public offering, making it one of India’s most heavily subscribed billion-dollar share sales.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
summary: Brent oil slipped after surging almost 6% over the previous two sessions, as traders weighed continued hostilities between the US and Iran against efforts to broker a new ceasefire.

title: Turkish Airlines Exploring Acquisitions in Asia, South America
summary: Turkish Airlines is exploring more acquisition opportunities as it looks to expand its global reach.

title: Japan Insurers Buy Most Super-Long Bonds in Three Years in June
summary: Japanese insurance firms bought the most super-long government bonds in three years last month, adding to signs that demand from a key buyer is stabilizing.

title: Iron Ore Drops as China Steel Losses Deepen, Hormuz Risks Rise
summary: Iron ore fell for a second day as deteriorating profit margins at Chinese steel mills and continued US-Iran hostilities clouded the demand outlook.

title: Global Investors Turn Cautious on Indian Bonds
summary: The rupee is also under strain, with slowing debt inflows coinciding with renewed foreign selling in stocks.

title: Moody’s Flags Rising Risks in Indonesia on Policy Uncertainty
summary: Moody’s Ratings remains concerned about policy uncertainty and fiscal sustainability risks in Indonesia, reinforcing its cautious outlook and warning that downside risks are likely to persist.

title: Korean Stock Traders Slash Margin Loans to Lowest Since April
summary: South Korean investors slashed their leveraged stock positions to the lowest level in three months, as memory-chip losses halted the market’s rally.

title: Global Stocks Lift Australian Pension Funds to 9.5% Annual Gain
summary: A rally in global equities delivered another strong year for Australia’s A$4.4 trillion ($3.1 trillion) pension fund industry, despite lingering concerns over the Middle East conflict and stubborn inflation.
